note_html:
Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Matthew, presents with a chronic history of back pain that has been exacerbated by recent weather changes and prolonged sitting. He reports difficulty standing and walking due to pain, with significant pain radiating down his left leg. He describes the pain as an electrical toothache and rates it as a 6 out of 10, but notes it can worsen to a 13 out of 10. He has been experiencing numbness and tingling in both legs, with weakness in both lower extremities. He has been bedridden for the past month and has been using a cane for support.

Past medical history: The patient has a history of a fall off a roof 30 years ago, which may have resulted in a fracture. He has also been treated for back pain in the past with NSAIDs and physical therapy (PT), but has not found relief.

Review of systems: The patient reports worsening pain with coughing, sneezing, and standing. He experiences relief with laying down.

Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]

Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]

Physical exam: The patient has a positive pain response to palpation at the L4 vertebrae, and experiences pain with flexion and extension of the back. Strength in the left leg is 4 out of 5, and 5 out of 5 in the right leg. Reflexes are brisk and motor and sensory are intact in both lower extremities.

Test Results: MRI shows a disk herniation at the level of L4-L5 vertebrae associated with nerve root impingement.

Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient presents with acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement causing lower back pain and leg pain.

Problem list: Acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement; chronic back pain; leg pain; numbness and tingling in both legs; weakness in both lower extremities.

Plan:
I recommended an epidural steroid injection to alleviate inflammation and referred the patient back to PT for strengthening exercises. I also advised the patient to see me again in a month to assess the effectiveness of the treatment.

Follow-up: I want the patient to see me again in about a month to reassess the effectiveness of the treatment plan.
